message:
chore(beads): track upstream path-dependency compile blockers

Add issue br-jtvzs documenting upstream compile failures in asupersync
and frankensqlite that block clean remote verification for recent fixes
in this repo. Includes initial impact mapping and technical analysis of
the asupersync rate_limit.rs blocker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1506,6 +1506,7 @@
 {"id":"br-i4ko","title":"T8.4: Implement remote endpoint/content check matrix for deployed bundles","description":"## Objective
Implement remote endpoint and content validation matrix for deployed static bundles.

## Work
- Probe baseline endpoints (`/`, `/viewer/`, `/manifest.json`) with expected status semantics.
- Validate presence/accessibility of critical viewer/data assets.
- Detect common deployment-path mismatches and produce actionable diagnostics.

## Deliverable
Deterministic remote availability/content checks with useful remediation messages.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:41.096419482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:31:08.501043533Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:31:08.501022914Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deploy","share","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-i4ko","depends_on_id":"br-1td4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-i4ko","depends_on_id":"br-2cph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1001,"issue_id":"br-i4ko","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Remote endpoint checks implemented: remote.root, remote.viewer, remote.manifest, remote.coop, remote.coep, remote.database, remote.content_match (SHA256), remote.tls. All integrated in run_verify_live(). 230 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:31:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-i8ci6","title":"Track C: Property-Based Testing","description":"**Background**

The project has 1000+ hand-written unit tests, but property-based testing can find edge cases that manual test authoring misses. The current test suite does not use proptest or any fuzzing framework.

**Graveyard reference:** Section 6.12 (Property-Based Testing). QuickCheck/proptest generates thousands of random inputs guided by properties (invariants) that must always hold. It has found bugs in production systems that billions of hand-written tests missed.

**EV calculation:** (severity=4 * breadth=5 * feasibility=5) / (risk=2 * effort=1) = 50.0 (highest EV in the entire queue)","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- proptest added to workspace dependencies with deterministic seed plumbing for CI replay
- Unit-level generator tests validate strategy correctness and model invariant compatibility
- Generators for core model types (ProjectRow, AgentRow, MessageRow and related envelopes)
- Property tests for cache, coalesce, queries, and TUI layout with explicit invariants documented
- At least 30 property tests total across modules with minimum case counts and shrink diagnostics
- Integration harness runs representative randomized scenarios against real subsystem boundaries
- E2E scripts consume generated edge-case fixtures to validate end-user behavior continuity
- Test output logs failing seeds, minimized counterexamples, and reproduction commands","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:27:00.949511329Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T09:27:00.949492394Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-i8ci6","depends_on_id":"br-11238","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-i8ci6","depends_on_id":"br-1j0z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-i8ci6","depends_on_id":"br-3cgci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-iqtm","title":"Add edge case tests: message-to-self, Unicode subject truncation, force-release idempotency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:42:19.957069131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:16:47.002929669Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T00:16:47.002911245Z","close_reason":"Implemented 7 edge case tests: message-to-self (DB), force-release idempotency (DB), release_reservations idempotency (DB), 4 Unicode subject prefix+truncation tests (messaging). Also fixed pre-existing clippy lint (unwrap_or_default in queries.rs).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"]}
+{"id":"br-jtvzs","title":"Track upstream path-dependency compile blockers that prevent clean rch verification","description":"As of 2026-03-11, bv robot triage reports no open/actionable repo-local beads, but clean remote verification for recent fixes is still blocked by upstream path-dependency compile failures outside this repo. Observed blockers: (1) /data/projects/asupersync/src/service/rate_limit.rs:354 -> error[E0277] '<S as Service<Request>>::Error cannot be unpinned' during remote verification of br-2g2dp; (2) /data/projects/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-wal/src/wal_fec.rs:1784 -> error[E0596] cannot borrow *receiver as mutable, as it is behind a & reference during remote verification of br-1591x. Objective: map which verification surfaces in this repo are blocked by each upstream failure, coordinate with other agents so only one person escalates/owns each blocker, and determine the highest-impact next fix path instead of leaving verification noise undocumented.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:25:20.072229823Z","created_by":"SunnyTurtle","updated_at":"2026-03-11T04:27:40.427767875Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1174,"issue_id":"br-jtvzs","author":"SunnyTurtle","text":"Initial impact map: the root workspace patches both asupersync and the frankensqlite/sqlmodel stack from local path dependencies. asupersync is the broader blocker inside this repo because it is a direct workspace dependency in mcp-agent-mail-db, storage, server, tools, CLI, and conformance, and it is patched workspace-wide in Cargo.toml. The frankensqlite failure enters primarily through mcp-agent-mail-db via sqlmodel-frankensqlite/fsqlite and then propagates to crates that depend on the DB layer (server, tools, storage, share, CLI, binary). Based on that blast radius, I’m treating the asupersync compile failure at /data/projects/asupersync/src/service/rate_limit.rs:354 as the first upstream target and the fsqlite-wal failure at /data/projects/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-wal/src/wal_fec.rs:1784 as secondary.","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:26:41Z"},{"id":1175,"issue_id":"br-jtvzs","author":"SunnyTurtle","text":"First technical lead on the asupersync side: /data/projects/asupersync/src/service/rate_limit.rs uses a pin_project enum future (RateLimitFuture<F, E>) with an Error(Option<RateLimitError<E>>) variant, while the neighboring middleware implementations that compile (load_shed, hedge, concurrency_limit) use simpler wrapper structs or an internal state enum/struct projection pattern. The current rate_limit impl still adds S::Future: Unpin, but the compiler complaint points at S::Error / the future type shape rather than the inner future poll path. Working hypothesis: the RateLimitFuture enum/projection shape is introducing an unnecessary Unpin requirement on the error-carrying variant, and the smallest correct upstream fix is likely to reshape it toward the load_shed/concurrency_limit state-machine pattern rather than just papering over it with a broader bound.","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:27:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-kqelk","title":"B.2: Wire ledger into backpressure, SLO, retention, and TUI diff decisions","description":"**Background**

The evidence ledger from B.1 is only useful if actual decisions are recorded into it. This task wires the ledger into the 5 highest-value decision points in the codebase:

1. **Cache eviction** (cache.rs) -- each eviction decision records: cache size, evicted key age, frequency, queue (small/main for S3-FIFO).
2. **Backpressure level** (if implemented in server) -- records: queue depth, recent latency p99, action (accept/defer/reject).
3. **Deferred touch flush** (cache.rs:502) -- records: shard count, pending count, elapsed since last flush, action (flush/defer).
4. **Coalesce join/timeout** (coalesce.rs:258) -- records: key, role (leader/joiner), timeout_ms, action outcome.
5. **TUI render strategy** (when D is implemented) -- records: frame state, budget remaining, action (incremental/full/deferred).

**Scope / Adoption wedge**

At each decision point, add a single `evidence_ledger().record(...)` call. This is a one-liner addition at each site. The evidence JSON is constructed from local variables already in scope.

For cache eviction (highest value): in the `lru_evict_if_full` function (or S3-FIFO equivalent), after each eviction:

```rust
evidence_ledger().record(
    "cache.eviction",
    serde_json::json!({
        "cache_size": map.len(),
        "evicted_age_secs": entry.inserted.elapsed().as_secs(),
        "evicted_freq": entry.access_count,
    }),
    "evict",
    Some("hit_rate >= 0.85"),
    0.9,
    "s3fifo_v1",
);
```

For coalesce outcomes: in `execute_or_join` after determining role:

```rust
evidence_ledger().record(
    "coalesce.outcome",
    serde_json::json!({
        "key_hash": hash(&key),
        "inflight_count": self.inflight_count(),
    }),
    if was_joined { "joined" } else { "executed" },
    Some("join_rate >= 0.3"),
    0.8,
    "coalesce_v1",
);
```

**Risks / Safe Mode**

- Risk: Adding overhead to hot paths. Mitigation: `record()` is < 1 microsecond (mutex + VecDeque push_back). Benchmark to confirm.
- Risk: serde_json::json! macro allocations. Mitigation: Only allocate in trace-level paths; use compile-time feature flag `evidence-ledger` to strip all recording calls in production if needed.
- Fallback trigger: If any benchmark shows > 2% regression from recording calls, add `#[cfg(feature = "evidence-ledger")]` around all call sites.

**Validation**

After wiring, run the existing stress tests and verify:
1. Evidence ledger has entries for all 5 decision points.
2. No test regression (all 1000+ tests pass).
3. Overhead measurement: insert 10K items into cache, measure total time with and without ledger recording; difference must be < 5%.

**Tests (5 required)**

1. `evidence_cache_eviction_recorded` -- trigger eviction, verify ledger has "cache.eviction" entry
2. `evidence_coalesce_outcome_recorded` -- run coalesce with joiners, verify entries
3. `evidence_deferred_flush_recorded` -- trigger flush, verify entry
4. `evidence_no_regression_cache_stress` -- 10K inserts, wall time < 1.05x baseline
5. `evidence_multiple_decision_points` -- exercise all 5 points, verify query returns distinct types","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Evidence recording hooks are added at all planned decision points (backpressure, SLO, retention, TUI diff, and one additional guard point)
- Each hook emits structured payloads with decision inputs, selected action, confidence/expected loss, and correlation ID
- Unit tests verify payload schema, redaction compliance, and behavior when ledger is disabled/unavailable
- Integration tests validate outcome-backfill linking and cross-component decision trace continuity
- E2E scenario exercises decision spikes and confirms ledger and runtime behavior remain stable
- Performance overhead remains within agreed budget and is evidenced by benchmark artifacts
- Diagnostic logs expose dropped-record reasons, write failures, and per-point emission counters","notes":"## Plan-Space Review Findings (2026-02-13)

**CRITICAL: 3 of 5 decision points don't exist yet.** Backpressure (#2) is not implemented. TUI render strategy (#5) requires Track D. Scope down to the 3 implementable points NOW:
1. Cache eviction (cache.rs) — EXISTS
2. Deferred touch flush (cache.rs:502) — EXISTS
3. Coalesce join/timeout (coalesce.rs:258) — EXISTS

Points 4-5 should be marked as "wire when available" with a TODO comment, not hard-required.

**CRITICAL: No global `evidence_ledger()` accessor defined.** B.1 defines the struct but not the singleton. Add to this bead's scope: `pub fn evidence_ledger() -> &'static EvidenceLedger { static LEDGER: OnceLock<EvidenceLedger> = OnceLock::new(); LEDGER.get_or_init(|| EvidenceLedger::new(1000)) }` in a new `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/evidence.rs` module.

**FIX: Snippet error.** The coalesce snippet uses `hash(&key)` which isn't a free function in Rust. Use `{ use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher, BuildHasher}; let mut h = std::hash::RandomState::new().build_hasher(); key.hash(&mut h); h.finish() }`.

**FIX: Test 5 is unimplementable.** `evidence_multiple_decision_points` says "exercise all 5 points" but only 3 exist. Change to "exercise all 3 implementable points."

**Additional test:** `evidence_record_overhead` — time 10K `record()` calls, verify p99 < 1 microsecond.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:43:34.756835820Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T17:43:34.756811364Z","close_reason":"Wired evidence ledger into 3 decision points: cache.eviction (TTL expiry in get_project), cache.deferred_flush (drain_touches), coalesce.outcome (leader/joiner). Added global evidence_ledger() singleton in core. 5 required tests passing (evidence_wiring.rs). All 472 db lib tests pass, zero clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-kqelk","depends_on_id":"br-35pui","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-kvtl7","title":"Track I: Quick Wins","description":"**Background**

Three low-effort, high-value optimizations that can be landed independently:

1. **Flat combining for cache flush** (R10, section 14.2): Replace 16 sequential shard locks in `has_pending_touches()` with an atomic flag. EV: 3.0.
2. **SQL template caching** (R11): Pre-compute stable SQL format strings with `OnceLock`. EV: 12.0.
3. **Contact query UNION** (R12): Combine 2 separate queries in `list_recent_contact_agent_ids()` into a single UNION query. EV: 15.0.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Atomic pending flag for cache flush check implemented with race-safe semantics
- OnceLock SQL templates applied to hot-path query construction without behavior change
- Contact lookup UNION refactor preserves exact functional output and ordering guarantees
- Unit tests added for each quick win plus invariants for regression-sensitive edge cases
- Integration tests validate quick wins together in realistic read/search/message workflows
- E2E regression script validates user-visible behavior parity before/after quick wins
- Performance improvements are measurable and captured with reproducible benchmark artifacts
- tracing diagnostics provide before/after counters and fast-failure breadcrumbs per optimization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:13:44.541618823Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:13:44.541541638Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-kvtl7","depends_on_id":"br-11fd5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-kvtl7","depends_on_id":"br-3ocb0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-kvtl7","depends_on_id":"br-3pns5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-l4uwn","title":"fetch_inbox returns empty despite successful send_message delivery","description":"## Summary\nIn TUI V2 E2E runs, send_message returns successful deliveries with payload IDs, but immediate and retried fetch_inbox calls for the recipient return [] for at least 5 attempts.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. register agents and set contact policy open.\n2. send_message from RedHawk -> BlueFalcon succeeds with deliveries payload id.\n3. fetch_inbox(project, BlueFalcon) returns [] repeatedly.\n\n## Evidence\n- tests/artifacts/tui_v2/20260212_155505/case2_send_toast_msg.json\n- tests/artifacts/tui_v2/20260212_155505/case2_inbox_attempt_*.json\n\n## Impact\n- Breaks inbox-based assertions in TUI E2E (toast/inbox/global inbox cases).\n\n## Notes\nLikely separate from set_contact_policy lookup regression (which is now fixed).","notes":"Investigation findings (2026-02-12):\n1) Original failing artifact remains valid: tests/artifacts/tui_v2/20260212_155505 case2 send succeeds and fetch_inbox returns []. Server log shows fetch query params [agent_id=2, project_id=1, limit=20] returning 0 rows immediately after insert.\n2) That artifact DB is malformed: sqlite3 /data/tmp/e2e_tui_v2.Nzy29w/storage.sqlite3 'PRAGMA integrity_check;' reports malformed image + widespread index inconsistencies. message_recipients COUNT(*) with index path returns 0 while NOT INDEXED scan returns 45.\n3) Fresh repro on current HEAD does NOT reproduce: full scripts/e2e_tui_v2.sh run at tests/artifacts/tui_v2/20260212_230311 passes case2/global inbox; send->fetch works on first attempt.\nConclusion: br-l4uwn symptom aligns with DB corruption state, not current fetch_inbox logic regression. Corruption/root-cause track remains br-3kwox.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CalmAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:58:27.132094702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T23:05:12.892020957Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T23:05:12.891999887Z","close_reason":"Non-repro on current HEAD; symptom traced to malformed artifact DB/index corruption (see br-3kwox).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1002,"issue_id":"br-l4uwn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reconfirmed on current rebuilt binary and live local server: send_message returns successful delivery payloads, but fetch_inbox for recipient remains [] after retries. Also observed while checking Agent Mail coordination inbox for CobaltOtter.","created_at":"2026-02-12T16:04:43Z"}]}
